findings.title: Test code in production: 2 issues
findings.description:
Test utilities and patterns found in what appears to be production code.

**Found:**
• Test code (lines 28, 43)
⚠️ This file is imported by production code!
findings.suggested_fix:
Options:

1. **Move to test files:** If this is test code, move it to a test directory
2. **Use TYPE_CHECKING:** For type hints only, wrap imports in `if TYPE_CHECKING:`
3. **Environment check:** If needed at runtime, guard with environment variable

```python
# Instead of:
from unittest.mock import Mock

# Use:
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING
if TYPE_CHECKING:
from unittest.mock import Mock
```
findings.why_it_matters: This test code is imported by production code. Test dependencies add bloat, security risks, and can cause unexpected behavior in production.

findings.title: Password may be logged in cleartext
findings.description:
Sensitive data (Password) appears in logging statement.

**Analysis:**
🔒 Credential type: Password
📦 In function: `main` (1 callers)
findings.suggested_fix: Remove sensitive data from logs or use masking.
findings.why_it_matters:
Credentials logged in cleartext can be:
• Exposed in log files accessible to attackers
• Sent to centralized logging systems
• Visible in monitoring dashboards
• Captured in crash reports

findings.title: Insecure TLS/Certificate Validation (kotlin)
findings.description:
**Custom trust manager (may bypass validation)** (CWE-295)

Disabled certificate or hostname verification detected.

**Pattern**: `X509TrustManager`
**File**: okhttp/src/androidMain/kotlin/okhttp3/internal/platform/android/StandardAndroidSocketAdapter.kt:48
**Code**: `X509TrustManager::class.java,`

This allows man-in-the-middle attacks. An attacker on the network can intercept and modify traffic without detection.
findings.suggested_fix: Use the default TrustManager and HostnameVerifier. For self-signed certs, add the CA to your trust store.

findings.title: Structural bridge: `samples/guide/src/main/java/okhttp3/recipes/PrintEvents.java` separates components of [192, 26]
findings.description: `samples/guide/src/main/java/okhttp3/recipes/PrintEvents.java` is a structural bridge. Removing it would split the graph into components of sizes [192, 26]. All communication between these components currently flows through this single node.
findings.suggested_fix: Reduce coupling through this node by introducing alternative call paths, extracting shared interfaces, or splitting the bridge function into independently accessible units.
findings.why_it_matters: A structural bridge is a single node whose failure disconnects entire subsystems. This creates fragile architecture where a bug, API change, or refactor in one function can cascade to all dependent components.

findings.title: Structural bridge: `/tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/okhttp/samples/guide/src/main/java/okhttp3/recipes/PrintEvents.java::PrintEvents.run:41` separates components of [190, 28]

findings.title: Hardcoded timeout: 3 seconds
findings.description:
Magic timeout value `3000` makes configuration and tuning difficult.

**Analysis:**
⏱️ Duration: 3 seconds
📍 Context: I/O operation
📦 In function: `run`
findings.suggested_fix:
Extract to a named constant:

```python
READ_TIMEOUT_MS = 3000  # 3 seconds
```
findings.why_it_matters: Hardcoded timeouts are hard to find, tune, and change across environments. Network timeouts especially need to be configurable based on deployment.
